部.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: phonetic 咅 (OC *pʰɯʔ) + semantic 邑; originally referring to a place in today's Gansu.
部.difference: Our note names 阜 as the phonetic component, but Wiktionary identifies the phonetic as 咅 (not 阜); semantic 邑 matches.

其.wiki_summary: Pictogram originally depicting a basket; 丌 (stand) was later added beneath, then the character was phonetically borrowed as a pronoun, with derivative 箕 retaining the original meaning.
其.difference: Wiktionary classifies 其 as a pictogram (basket shape) that was phonetically borrowed, not a phono-semantic compound.

些.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound composed of 此 above 二; the etymology text is otherwise marked incomplete on Wiktionary.

主.wiki_summary: Pictogram depicting a lamp or torch with a flame at the top; original form of 炷.

樣.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 木 (wood) + phonetic 羕 (OC *laŋs).

理.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 玉 (jade) + phonetic 里; original meaning was 'to polish jade' per Shuowen Jiezi.

心.wiki_summary: Pictogram depicting a heart, now highly stylized; derives from Proto-Sino-Tibetan *səm.

她.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 女 (female) + phonetic 也 (OC *laːlʔ); repurposed c. 1910s as a feminine third-person pronoun.

本.wiki_summary: Ideogram (指事): tree 木 with an extra stroke at the bottom highlighting the roots.

前.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: originally 歬, combining 止 (foot) and 舟 (boat) to represent a foot on a boat moving forward; 刀 was later added, making this the original form of 剪.
前.difference: Wiktionary classifies 前 as an ideogrammic compound (止 + 舟), not a phono-semantic compound; our note incorrectly identifies 止 as the phonetic component.
